parameters.content:
### Every Few Minutes

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 10 min. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Fetch Current Metrics

### Fetch Current Metrics

GET https://api.example.com/v1/assets — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Compute Severity Signals · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Compute Severity Signals

Custom JavaScript: Compute a simple moving average and momentum signal

Next: Grade Severity Tier

### Grade Severity Tier

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: info, warning, critical + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Just Record (Info) · ②→ Warn Channel (Slack) · ③→ Page Urgently (Slack) · ④→ Archive Unscored

### Just Record (Info)

Appends a new row to 'Asset Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Warn Channel (Slack)

Posts a message to Slack (`#trading`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Page Urgently (Slack)

Posts a message to Slack (`#trading`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Archive Unscored

Appends a new row to 'Asset Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Tier Outcomes

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Guard Non-Empty price

### Guard Non-Empty price

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#trading`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#trading`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Alert on market moves with HTTP / REST API and Slack

**Pattern:** Trading Alert · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Alert on market moves with HTTP / REST API and Slack. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual portfolio tracking is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Every Few Minutes → Fetch Current Metrics → Compute Severity Signals → Grade Severity Tier → Just Record (Info) → Merge Tier Outcomes → Guard Non-Empty price → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to trading alert without manual effort
- Anyone automating portfolio tracking in finance
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Scales without extra headcount
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Three response tiers, not a single yes/no alert, match the response to the severity — logging every metric quietly would bury the one critical spike, and paging on-call for every info-level blip trains them to ignore alerts entirely. Critical gets a distinct, louder channel from warning on purpose.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Adjust the tier thresholds and see how alert sensitivity changes.
2. Trigger a critical-tier value and confirm it pages a different channel than warning does.
3. Add a 4th tier between warning and critical.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your portfolio tracking process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
